channel.title: NPR Topics: Technology
channel.description: Latest technology news and breakthroughs in technology, science, and industry. Download the NPR Technology podcast and Technology RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Scientists make a pocket-sized AI brain with help from monkey neurons
item.description: A new study suggests AI systems could be a lot more efficient. Researchers were able to shrink an AI vision model to 1/1000th of its original size.
item.content:encoded:
A new study suggests AI systems could be a lot more efficient. Researchers were able to shrink an AI vision model to 1/1000th of its original size.

(Image credit: AerialPerspective Images)
item.dc:creator: Jon Hamilton

item.title: College students, professors are making their own AI rules. They don't always agree
item.description: More than three years after ChatGPT debuted, AI has become a part of everyday life — and professors and students are still figuring out how or if they should use it.
item.content:encoded: More than three years after ChatGPT debuted, AI has become a part of everyday life — and professors and students are still figuring out how or if they should use it.
item.dc:creator: Lee V. Gaines

item.title: How long do electric vehicle batteries actually last?
item.description: When the modern electric vehicle was still in its infancy, drivers worried that vehicles would need expensive battery replacements within a few years. But battery lifespans are exceeding expectations.
item.content:encoded:
When the modern electric vehicle was still in its infancy, drivers worried that vehicles would need expensive battery replacements within a few years. But battery lifespans are exceeding expectations.

(Image credit: Odd Andersen)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: Prediction market trader 'Magamyman' made $553,000 on death of Iran's supreme leader
item.description: It's the latest trade drawing scrutiny on the popular prediction market site for appearing to show an insider making profits on military secrets.
item.content:encoded:
It's the latest trade drawing scrutiny on the popular prediction market site for appearing to show an insider making profits on military secrets.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: How the federal government is painting immigrants as criminals on social media
item.description: Experts say this kind of media campaign is unprecedented and paints a distorted picture of immigrants and crime
item.content:encoded: Experts say this kind of media campaign is unprecedented and paints a distorted picture of immigrants and crime
item.dc:creator: Huo Jingnan

item.title: OpenAI announces Pentagon deal after Trump bans Anthropic
item.description: Shortly after the president's ban of artificial intelligence company Anthropic, rival OpenAI announced it had done a deal with the Defense Department to provide its technology for classified networks.
item.content:encoded:
Shortly after the president's ban of artificial intelligence company Anthropic, rival OpenAI announced it had done a deal with the Defense Department to provide its technology for classified networks.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: Shannon Bond

item.title: What to know about the showdown between AI company Anthropic and the Pentagon
item.description: NPR speaks with Maria Curi, an AI tech policy reporter at Axios, about the showdown between Anthropic and the Department of Defense.
item.content:encoded: NPR speaks with Maria Curi, an AI tech policy reporter at Axios, about the showdown between Anthropic and the Department of Defense.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Deadline looms as Anthropic rejects Pentagon demands it remove AI safeguards
item.description: The Defense Department has been feuding with Anthropic over military uses of its artificial intelligence tools. At stake are hundreds of millions of dollars in contracts and access to some of the most advanced AI on the planet.
item.content:encoded:
The Defense Department has been feuding with Anthropic over military uses of its artificial intelligence tools. At stake are hundreds of millions of dollars in contracts and access to some of the most advanced AI on the planet.

(Image credit: Patrick Sison)
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: These major issues have brought together Democrats and Republicans in states
item.description: Across the country, Republicans and Democrats have found bipartisan agreement on regulating artificial intelligence and data centers. But it's not just big tech aligning the two parties.
item.content:encoded:
Across the country, Republicans and Democrats have found bipartisan agreement on regulating artificial intelligence and data centers. But it's not just big tech aligning the two parties.

(Image credit: Ted Shaffrey)
item.dc:creator: Regan McCarthy

item.title: Secretly rewritten nuclear safety rules are made public
item.description: The Energy Department made the rules public a month after NPR reported about their existence. The rules slash requirements for security and environmental protections.
item.content:encoded:
The Energy Department made the rules public a month after NPR reported about their existence. The rules slash requirements for security and environmental protections.

(Image credit: Idaho National Laboratory)
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel
